“Le mathematicien invente des outils conceptuels pour percevoir la realite. Une realite qui est la […] plus fondamentale que la nature.” Alain Connes

Mathematics

I note the difficulties I had to approach these topics with 🔥 . None: accessible to anyone after high-school. 1 🔥 2 years of undergrad studies in mathematics needed, 2 🔥🔥 up to beginning of master (I am consolidating), more 🔥🔥🔥🔥 grad studies and beyond (so things I do not master).

Intuitions

  • Famous and excellent videos: 3Blue1Brown

Differential Geometry

We usually learn a tone of key concepts in Linear algebra, buidling the first blocks of an abstract reasoning in mathematics. To me this was thanks to my amazing teacher in Stanislas (thank you Pr. Etrog!) and latter on Ecole Polytechnique. But I never got the big picture of what we were trying to build, maybe because of vocabulary? For example “topological” is a very intriguing words at first, we can easily put a lot of meaning and misconceptions on it before understanding. Trying to obtain results on more abstract and general spaces is one way to derive an intuitions and get the vocabulary. Another is to progressively build from zero tools that will be the building stones of a field. When one wish to study Differential Geometry, it first requires to build sets, then topological spaces, then manifolds. If well presented I fell that we get through this construction an hint of what it means to do Math. This was exactly achieved by Frederic Schuller, in the following lectures.

  • 🔥 I have followed this course about differential geometry, made by Frederic Schuller, I would really recommend it to anyone, it is very clear and well explained with rigor. Open my eyes on how to approach Math again. Lectures on the Geometric Anatomy of Theoretical Physics Did you know that “a lady should never visit the cemetery …. of matrices ?

  • 🔥🔥

  • (French) this introduction by Alain Connes is very interesting talk on how very abstract Mathematical theory influences our understanding of Physics and other sciences. Les mathematiques et la pensee en mouvement Has the time arrived to do the same with Neuroscience?

Information Geometry

The field of information geometry merges the tools of Differential Geometry and Information Theory (first built by Shannon). It presents a geometrical interpretation of probability distributions and explain how we can interpret statistical models with geometry. Questions that can be adressed with such tools are the following: What is the right gradient to use when optimising a neural network? What does it mean for a system to be optimal?
Some researchers think it is a really attracting fields for theoretical Neuroscience. I am also trying to discover it. What is the promise? We discovered that in many parts of the brains, the firing of a population of cells is constrained on a manifold. To be more precise: the population activity vectors (a n-dimensional vector for a system of n-neurons, each dimension representing the firing rate of each neuron) are constrained on the manifold. One example is the head direction system which is constrained to a ring. So maybe there is a way to bridge a link between this manifold, and the ones that are considered in Information Geometry? Then we could use all the tools that were developed, at least theoretically.

  • A first good reading is the Mathematical Theory of Communication by Claude E. Shannon and Warren Weaver. It is really well explained and some examples are astounding. Shannon had in mind a framework built on channels and sources. I wonder if it is readily applicable to a mathematical theory on spaces that are a bit weird?

  • 🔥 To dive directly into the field, I would recomment this introduction by Frank Nielsen, which then pointis to further referencec. An elementary introduction to information geometry a first training in Differential Geometry is necessary.

  • 🔥 Another introduction, more easy to follow: Information Geometry at Microsoft Research

  • 🔥 I really enjoy the work and presentations of Yann Olivier, always of qualities: his website
